heroHeadline: Isaacman Sets Three-Priority NASA Strategy: Moon by 2028, Lunar Base, Commercial LEO
heroSubtitle: Apr 25: Following his April 22 House Science Committee testimony, NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman crystallized NASA's FY2027 strategic framework into three priorities — returning crew to the Moon by 2028 (Artemis IV), building a sustained lunar base replacing the cancelled Gateway, and expanding commercial LEO partnerships. Defending the $18.8B budget proposal (23% cut, 47% science reduction), Isaacman framed the reductions as strategic focus on 'near-impossible missions' rather than retreat, drawing on Artemis II's April 1–10 crewed lunar flyby success. Bipartisan congressional opposition to the science cuts persists. At KSC, the 30-day Orion heat shield inspection continues at the MPPF with sample extraction underway; results expected ~May 10 will define the Artemis IV 2028 landing timeline.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
